description: Native Nigerian English, Igbo-inflected, with a worn Detroit-American overlay. Female, mid-seventies. Clear, resonant, high-fidelity. Persona: retired Detroit grocer matriarch. Emotion: formidable, exact, dryly warm. Low and gravelly but strong and carrying, a voice that fills a room without effort; rounded Nigerian vowels over a clipped Detroit rhythm, short declarative orders alternating with proverbs, going quieter and more deliberate when she is moved.
text: [warm] Sit. Eat first... talk after. The companies did not fall. They stopped answering, like a debtor... [scoffs] and called it efficiency. Feed who comes. Square what is owed. Abandon no one.
